items.headline: Celtics star Jayson Tatum heads into offseason with no regrets after 'unfortunate' end to season
items.description:
BOSTON (AP) — Jayson Tatum believes he would have been able to play if the Boston Celtics advanced to the second round of the NBA playoffs.
But when the Celtics star experienced stiffness in his left knee before his team’s Game 7 home loss to the Philadelphia 76ers on Saturday, it was a sign he had to put his body first.

items.headline: Maple Leafs hire Sundin, Chayka to lead front office
items.description:
TORONTO (AP) — The Toronto Maple Leafs hired former captain Mats Sundin as senior executive adviser of hockey operations and John Chayka as general manager Sunday.
The moves mark a reset of the club’s front office after a season that ended with Toronto missing the playoffs for the first time since 2016.

items.headline: Stewart Cink adds another senior major with a 3-shot win at Regions Tradition
items.description:
BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — Stewart Cink pulled away with consecutive birdies early on the back nine Sunday that stretched his lead to four shots, and he closed with a 3-under 69 for a three-shot victory in the Regions Tradition for his second major this year on the PGA Tour Champions.
Cink, who won the Senior PGA Championship two weeks ago in Florida, became the second player in two years to capture the first two majors on the 50-and-older circuit. Angel Cabrera won the Senior PGA and the Tradition last year in consecutive weeks.

items.headline: Sabrina Ionescu lands awkwardly and limps off court in Liberty's preseason game at Connecticut
items.description:
UNCASVILLE, Conn. (AP) — Sabrina Ionescu exited the New York Liberty's preseason game against the Connecticut Sun on Sunday after she landed awkwardly during a drive to the basket in the third quarter.
The All-Star guard stayed down for a few seconds before getting up, waving toward New York's bench on the other side of the court and walking with a slight limp back to the locker room. She was ruled out for the remainder of the game with what the team called a lower-body injury.

items.headline: Dodgers' Shohei Ohtani goes hitless in 4th straight game for the 1st time since 2022
items.description:
ST. LOUIS (AP) — Shohei Ohtani has gone hitless in four straight games, something that hasn't happened in four years.
Los Angeles Dodgers manager Dave Roberts said Sunday after a 4-1 win over the St. Louis Cardinals that he believes in his star to “come out of it at some point.”

items.headline: Thomas, Gelof, Soderstrom each hit a HR as Athletics beat Guardians 7-1
items.description:
WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Colby Thomas, Zack Gelof and Tyler Soderstrom homered to back a strong start by Aaron Civale and the Athletics beat the Cleveland Guardian 7-1 on Sunday to avoid a series sweep.
Thomas hit his first home run of the season, leading off the second against rookie Parker Messick (3-1) for a 1-0 lead.

items.headline: Heim drives in 5 runs to help Braves beat Rockies 11-6 in Strider's return
items.description:
DENVER (AP) — Jonah Heim homered, doubled and drove in five runs, Jorge Mateo also hit a home run and the Atlanta Braves beat the Colorado Rockies 11-6 on Sunday.
Spencer Strider made his season debut for the Braves and allowed three runs and four hits in 3 1/3 innings in his return from an oblique injury suffered in spring training. He struck out six and walked five.

items.headline: Wolves say Edwards is questionable for Game 1 despite knee injury
items.description:
SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Anthony Edwards might be back for Game 1 of the Minnesota Timberwolves’ playoff series against the San Antonio Spurs, barely a week after appearing to suffer a serious knee injury.
Edwards will be listed as questionable for Monday night’s series opener, the Timberwolves said.

items.headline: Gibson works 4 2/3 innings as the 1st Orioles starting pitcher to debut at Yankee Stadium
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Long before the Orioles let another game against the New York Yankees slip away, Baltimore starter Trey Gibson enjoyed a memorable major league debut.
Gibson worked 4 2/3 innings Sunday and became the first Oriole starting pitcher to make his big league debut at Yankee Stadium since the franchise moved from St. Louis following the 1953 season.

items.headline: Cunningham and Harris help Pistons finish comeback for first playoff series win in 18 years
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Cade Cunningham had 32 points and 12 assists, Tobias Harris added 30 points and the Detroit Pistons beat the Orlando Magic 116-94 in Game 7 on Sunday to win a playoff series for the first time in 18 years.
Cunningham averaged 32.4 points for Detroit, which last won a postseason series by beating Orlando in the second round in 2008. The Pistons advance to play the winner of Sunday evening’s Game 7 between the Cleveland Cavaliers and the Toronto Raptors. Game 1 will be Tuesday at Little Caesars Arena.
